title: UpCadhoc Redemption
description: Where can I use my UpCadhoc gift card or voucher in France?
narrative: The user has received an UpCadhoc gift card or voucher and wants to know which physical stores, online retailers, or service providers accept it across France. They need a comprehensive, up-to-date list of partner merchants categorized by sector (e.g., fashion, beauty, leisure) and location (nearby or by postal code), preferably with a map interface. Relevance is determined by accuracy of merchant listings, inclusion of both national and local partners, and availability of search filters by category or geography.

title: Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: What are the traditional and variations of Moroccan lamb tagine recipes, including key ingredients, cooking methods, and common substitutions?
narrative: The user seeks authentic and adapted recipes for Moroccan lamb tagine, focusing on traditional preparations with spices like saffron, ras el hanout, and preserved lemon, as well as common regional or personal variations such as substituting potatoes with rice or adding mushrooms. Relevance is determined by recipes that include step-by-step instructions, authentic spice blends, and user-reported modifications that reflect cultural or dietary adaptations. Recipes that omit key traditional elements or lack cooking details are less relevant.

title: Used Sports Car
description: What are the best low-mileage, well-maintained used sports cars from the 1990s to early 2000s with verified service history and no accident records?
narrative: The user is seeking a premium or collectible sports car, such as a Lotus Elise, with low mileage (under 100,000 km), from the 1990s to early 2000s, that has a clean history, documented maintenance, and no salvage or accident records. They prioritize vehicles listed on reputable French platforms with access to official history reports like Histovec, and are wary of scams such as odometer tampering. Relevance is determined by verified vehicle history, low mileage, model prestige, and availability of service documentation—not just price or age.

title: Veal Paupiettes
description: What is a simple, family-friendly recipe for baked veal paupiettes with customizable sauce options and side pairings?
narrative: The user seeks a reliable, easy-to-follow recipe for veal paupiettes that accommodates personal preferences—such as substituting broth types, adding cream or herbs, or using wine instead of water—and includes suggested side dishes like potatoes, green beans, or pasta salad. Relevance is determined by recipes that provide clear step-by-step instructions, mention common variations from user comments, and emphasize simplicity, family appeal, and flavor enhancements without requiring advanced culinary skills.

title: Car Sun Protection
description: What are the most effective and legal methods to protect a car's interior and occupants from sun damage, heat, and UV radiation while parked or driving?
narrative: The user seeks practical, cost-effective, and legally compliant solutions to shield a vehicle from prolonged sun exposure, particularly for cars parked outdoors year-round. Relevance is determined by methods that address UV protection, heat reduction, interior preservation, and driver/occupant comfort—such as solar-tinted films (complying with 70% TLV on front windows), sun visors, shade strategies, and anti-humidity treatments. Solutions must distinguish between permanent installations (e.g., films) and temporary accessories (e.g., electrostatic visors), and prioritize safety, legality, and maintenance impact. Documents discussing rodent damage or battery care are irrelevant unless directly tied to sun/heat exposure effects.

title: Petit Bateau Apparel

narrative: The user is seeking information about Petit Bateau's women's clothing line, particularly its signature casual chic styles such as T-shirts, navy sweaters, waterproof wax jackets, and organic cotton underwear. title: Renault Employee Car Deal

title: War School E-books

title: Unemployment Option
description: How can a jobseeker in France choose between resuming old unemployment benefits or switching to new benefits based on recent employment, and what are the financial and procedural implications?
narrative: The user is a former unemployment benefit recipient who has returned to work and is now unemployed again. They want to maximize their financial support by understanding whether to exhaust their remaining old benefits, reload benefits based on new work, or exercise the 'right of option' to immediately switch to higher benefits tied to their most recent job. Relevance depends on accurate details about eligibility thresholds (e.g., 130 days/910 hours of work), the 30% increase or €20/day condition, the 21-day decision window, and the irreversible nature of the choice. The user needs clear, actionable guidance to avoid financial loss from suboptimal selection.

title: AXA Client Access
description: How can policyholders and banking customers securely access their AXA client spaces for insurance and banking services?
narrative: The user needs clear, authoritative guidance on accessing their AXA client area, distinguishing between insurance (axa.fr) and banking (axabanque.fr) portals, recovering lost credentials, and verifying website security to avoid phishing. Relevance is determined by accurate instructions for login procedures, official URLs, security best practices, and support channels—excluding third-party help sites or outdated information. title: Electric Car Incentives
description: What government incentives and financial aids are available for purchasing new or used electric vehicles in France, and how do they affect overall cost compared to conventional cars?
narrative: The user is considering purchasing an electric vehicle in France and wants to understand the full range of financial incentives—such as the ecological bonus, conversion premium, retrofit aid, and registration tax exemption—to determine the true net cost difference between electric and internal combustion engine vehicles. Information about battery costs, charging, or technical specs is irrelevant unless tied directly to purchase incentives.

title: Downy Mildew Safety
description: Are tomatoes and potatoes affected by downy mildew safe to eat, store, or use for seeding, and what precautions should be taken?
narrative: The user is a home gardener who has harvested tomatoes and potatoes showing signs of downy mildew and wants to know whether consuming, canning, or saving seeds from these crops poses health risks. They seek practical, science-based guidance on food safety, storage, and future planting. Relevant information includes expert opinions that symptomatic parts are not toxic but unappetizing, USDA warnings against canning diseased tomatoes, the safety of saving tomato seeds (but not potato tubers), and the importance of sanitation to prevent disease recurrence. Relevance is determined by whether the source addresses edibility, preservation methods, seed viability, and agricultural best practices specific to downy mildew-infected produce.

title: Three Days of Darkness
description: What are the prophesied causes and signs of the 'Three Days of Darkness' event, and how do they connect to cosmic phenomena, pole shifts, and global upheaval according to esoteric and religious sources?
narrative: The user seeks information on the 'Three Days of Darkness' prophecy as described across multiple traditions—including Padre Pio, Hopi, Maya, Velikovsky, and modern channelings like Monique Mathieu—to understand whether it is framed as a divine purification, a cosmic event triggered by Nibiru or planetary disruption, or a metaphor for spiritual awakening. Relevance is determined by sources that explicitly link the event to astronomical phenomena (e.g., celestial bodies blocking sunlight), geophysical changes (pole shift, earthquakes), and moral or spiritual consequences (human ignorance, societal collapse). Sources must reference historical or religious prophecies, not purely fictional or satirical interpretations. Claims must be tied to credible-seeming esoteric or prophetic traditions, even if scientifically implausible, to match the user’s interest in apocalyptic symbolism and cross-cultural parallels.

title: Clandestine Espionage

title: Cultural Immersion

title: Diarrhea Diet
description: What foods and beverages are recommended to manage diarrhea and support intestinal recovery?
narrative: The user is seeking dietary guidance for managing acute diarrhea, focusing on foods that soothe the digestive tract, minimize transit acceleration, and prevent dehydration. Relevant products include cooked, low-fiber starches like mashed potatoes (e.g., Black Truffle Potato Crush, though truffle flavor may be too strong), steamed potatoes, white rice, and cooked vegetables such as carrots and zucchini. Foods to avoid include raw produce, high-fiber items, dairy (except hard cheeses), fatty meats, and sugary drinks. The user prioritizes practical, easily prepared meals with clear nutritional rationale, as advised by a dietitian. Relevance is determined by alignment with evidence-based low-residue, low-fiber, and hydrating dietary recommendations for gastrointestinal distress.

narrative: The user is looking to switch electricity suppliers and prioritizes transparency in pricing, 100% renewable energy sourcing, and digital tools to monitor and optimize consumption in real time. title: Gallic Ancestors
description: What is the historical accuracy of the French national myth that modern French people descend primarily from the ancient Gauls?
narrative: The user seeks to understand how the popular notion of 'our ancestors the Gauls' emerged as a national narrative in France, and whether it reflects historical reality or political mythmaking. Relevant information includes the origins of the term 'Gallic' as used by Julius Caesar, the diversity of pre-Roman tribes in Gaul, the deliberate promotion of this myth in 19th-century French school textbooks to foster national unity, and the recognition today that modern French ancestry is multi-ethnic, incorporating Franks, Romans, Vikings, and waves of colonial and post-colonial immigrants. A document is relevant if it critically examines the construction, dissemination, or debunking of this myth, rather than merely describing ancient Gaulish tribes or Roman conquests without addressing the modern national narrative.

title: Algarve Car Rental

title: Potato Disease Management

title: Women in Rural History
description: What were the lived experiences of women farmers in mid-20th century France, and how were their voices preserved in archival records?
narrative: The user seeks to understand the daily lives and social contributions of rural women in France during the 1960s–1970s, particularly through primary sources such as letters archived by Menie Grégoire and the Departmental Archives of Indre-et-Loire. Documents that contextualize these women within broader gender and labor history—such as those tied to International Women’s Day initiatives or Wikipedia edit-a-thons focused on forgotten female figures—are highly relevant. title: Potato Cooking Methods

title: APRE vs. Back-to-Work Bonus
description: What are the differences between France's APRE personalized back-to-work assistance and the discontinued back-to-work bonus, and who qualifies for each?
narrative: The user is seeking to understand the distinction between two French financial aids for RSA beneficiaries returning to work: the currently active, departmentally-managed APRE (which covers itemized costs like transport, training, or childcare up to €3,000/year) and the now-abolished back-to-work bonus (a flat €1,000 payment for those with 4+ months of employment). They need clarity on eligibility criteria, funding sources, application processes, and geographic variability to determine which support they or someone they assist may qualify for—especially since APRE availability varies by department and the bonus no longer exists. Relevance hinges on accurate, up-to-date comparisons of structure, scope, and administrative rules for each program.
